# Governance Directives

Follow these directives in every file you write or edit.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

These are invariants. Violation is never acceptable.

_No invariants captured yet. Run `/edikt:invariant:new` to capture a hard constraint._

## Routing Table

Before starting a task, scan this table. If your task matches any signals or scopes, read the referenced file.

| Signals | Scope | File |
|---|---|---|
| `.kt`/`.kts` files, Kotlin idioms, null safety, `data class`, sealed types, coroutines, `Dispatchers`, Spring constructor injection, Axon commands/events | implementation, review | `governance/kotlin.md` |
| `*.gradle.kts`, `settings.gradle.kts`, `gradle/libs.versions.toml`, dependency declarations, plugin application, version catalog, lock files, configuration cache, convention plugins, `buildSrc/` | implementation, review | `governance/gradle.md` |
| `@RestController`/`@Controller`, `@ConfigurationProperties`, `@Document`, `@Profile`, `application*.yml`, Spring Data Mongo, Bean Validation, `@RestControllerAdvice`, Actuator, `@SpringBootTest` / `@WebMvcTest` / `@DataMongoTest` | implementation, review | `governance/spring-boot.md` |

## Reminders

Before acting, check the relevant constraint.

[edikt:reminders:start]: #
- Before declaring a Kotlin variable → reach for `val` first; use `var` only if reassignment is required by control flow (ref: kotlin)
- Before catching an exception → catch the specific subtype, never `Exception` / `Throwable` / `RuntimeException` in business logic (ref: kotlin)
- Before adding a dependency → declare its coordinates and version in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`, never inline in a build script (ref: gradle)
- Before declaring a dependency in a module → use `implementation` unless the type is part of the module's public API; reserve `api` for re-exported types (ref: gradle)
- Before binding configuration → use a typed `@ConfigurationProperties` class; never scatter `@Value` or read `System.getenv` directly (ref: spring-boot)
- Before adding a `@RestController` or `@Document` → place it under the correct hexagonal adapter (`adapter/in/web` or `adapter/out/readmodel`) (ref: spring-boot)
[edikt:reminders:end]: #

## Verification Checklist

Before finishing, verify each item. If any fails, fix before submitting.

- [ ] No `!!` non-null assertions outside test code (`grep -RInE '!!' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)
- [ ] No `GlobalScope.launch` / `GlobalScope.async` in application code (`grep -RIn 'GlobalScope' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)
- [ ] No Spring field injection via `@Autowired lateinit var` (`grep -RInE '@Autowired[[:space:]]+lateinit' src/main`) (ref: kotlin)
- [ ] No hardcoded versions in `build.gradle.kts` — every coordinate is referenced via `libs.` from the catalog (ref: gradle)
- [ ] No legacy `apply plugin:` or `buildscript { classpath(...) }` syntax in any `*.gradle.kts` file (ref: gradle)
- [ ] No dynamic versions (`+`, `latest.release`, `1.2.+`) in `gradle/libs.versions.toml` or build scripts (ref: gradle)
- [ ] No `@Value("${...}")` annotations outside a `@ConfigurationProperties` class (ref: spring-boot)
- [ ] No `@RestController` / `@Controller` / `@Document` annotations outside the corresponding `adapter/in/web/**` or `adapter/out/readmodel/**` packages (ref: spring-boot)
- [ ] No `management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: "*"` and no exposure of `env`/`configprops`/`heapdump`/`threaddump` on any public listener in `application*.yml` (ref: spring-boot)

## Reminder: Non-Negotiable Constraints

These constraints were listed above and are restated for emphasis.
Do not violate them under any circumstances.

_No invariants captured yet._
